title: The Strait of Hormuz crisis has exposed how fragile the world economy really is
standfirst: A conflict once framed as a regional test of force is now rippling into oil markets, inflation, aviation and shipping from Europe to the United States. The immediate danger is not only military escalation, but the possibility that a chokepoint through which a large share of the world’s energy trade flows becomes a lever in a broader war of attrition.
